TextENGNew: A cold, clammy silence filled the room.
Even though I was in my own room, I couldn't sit still, restlessly pacing about.
Rena sat perched by the window, gazing out at the village as night's curtain descended.
We had gathered at my house to put our 'plan' together.
But Shion alone had yet to show up, despite the appointed time having long since passed.
Shion couldn't be planning to betray us at the last moment... could she?
Suspicion rose in my mind. I clawed at my hair, trying to shake it off.
A dull pain throbbed at the back of my skull. Strangely, it helped distract me.
``Your tea's getting cold. You should drink it.``
``My mom and dad are both out.@k@vS18/01/160100308. I tried making it myself by watching how it's done, so the taste should be fine, probably.``
``It's some stuff my relatives sent us.@k@vS18/01/160100310. Apparently it's pretty popular in Tokyo.``
Rena said nothing, merely shifting her gaze silently toward the window.
I sat there by myself, sipping tea like an idiot.
No matter how much I drank, the dryness in my throat wouldn't go away.
``Shii-chan is late...``
Rena murmured, almost to herself.
Silence again.
Rena slowly ran her tongue over her dry lips.
The moist sound it made was strangely vivid.
Unable to sit still any longer, I got to my feet.
-- At the exact same moment, Rena whipped around with startling speed.
``Where are you going?``
Rena's piercing gaze stopped me in my tracks.
``Just going to check outside.@k@vS18/01/160100313. Maybe Shion got lost or something.``
``Shii-chan has been to your house before, hasn't she, Keiichi-kun?``
Rena kept her eyes locked on me, inching closer step by step.
``Maybe Shii-chan got cold feet? Got cold feet?``
Rena spoke as though she could see right through me.
``No way...@k@vS18/01/160100316. I can't imagine Shion would break a promise...``
``What if Shii-chan isn't the person you think she is, Keiichi-kun?``
``What are you saying...?@k@vS18/01/160100318. Doubting your own allies -- that's not right, Rena...``
``Rena trusts Keiichi-kun.``
``O-of course.``
``...So you absolutely must not betray me.``
Rena's hand clamped down on my arm.
``I know. I promise.``
``No matter what.``
Rena's grip tightened with crushing force.
Enough to hurt.
``No matter what...``
I barely managed to force the words out, nodding again and again.
``Rena will absolutely never betray Keiichi-kun either.@k@vS18/02/160200169. And if I ever break that promise...@k@vS18/02/160200170. Then it'll be Rena's turn to be punished by Oyashiro-sama.``
Saying that, Rena smiled sweetly.
When she finally released my arm, a vivid red imprint of her palm remained on my skin.
That mark, like a brand, throbbed with heat.
I had the sudden thought... that this mark might never fade.
We were about to set a terrible 'plan' into motion.
Suppose the 'plan' was carried out without a hitch.
Even so...
Our complicity would bind us together for the rest of our lives.
If any one of us let the secret slip, our entire relationship would shatter.
To keep from walking the road to ruin, we would watch each other, keep each other in check.
That tension would continue to shackle us until the day we died...
An unimaginably long span of years. I would carry this burden for my entire life.
A mark carved so deep it would never disappear...
Could my heart endure that without breaking...?
``Even if Keiichi-kun were to transfer to another school...@k@vS18/02/160200172. Rena would come find you.@k@vS18/02/160200173. No matter where you go.``
changeReason: Old preserves JPN meaning 'so you will never forget about Rena' (圭一くんがレナのこと、忘れてしまわないように); new replaces with 'no matter where you go', losing the fear of being forgotten.

TextENGNew: As if she could see right through my wavering heart, Rena spoke.
``But you don't need to worry.``
``I'm not worried.``
``That's right.@k@vS18/02/160200176. Because no matter how far people from Hinamizawa may go... they always come back.@k@vS18/02/160200177. Always.``
The sound of my own swallow echoed with raw clarity.
``So there's nothing to worry about, Keiichi-kun.``
Rena's words carved deep into my heart.
There was no turning back...
Rena's eyes held me captive, refusing to let go. I couldn't look away.
I could no longer run...
Every retreat had been cut off.
I had already set foot on the path where dark sins writhed and squirmed.
changeReason: Old says 'crawling down this road, time to walk on my own two feet' (positive determination); new says 'set foot on the path where dark sins writhed' (ominous). JPN 業が蠢く describes sins/karma squirming, matching the new translation's darker framing.

TextENGNew: The doorbell suddenly rang from the entryway.
Rena gave a quiet nod.
I bit my lip and opened the door to my room.
Then, as if confirming my own resolve with each step, I slowly descended the stairs.
One step at a time...
*Creak*...
The cold wood underfoot and the groaning sound it made.
Step by step, I descended, verifying with each one.
This path I was on.
There was no other path.
Don't look back.
You're on the right path.
A strange sense of mission filled me.
I felt no fear.
I would follow this path.
And I would open that door.
On the other side, our 'rightful future' was waiting!
``Kei-chan...?``
When I threw the front door open, Shion stared at my face with a look of bewilderment.
Her eyes were round, as though she were looking at a stranger.
``Somehow... you look like a completely different person...``
Shion tried to cover it with a smile, but she clearly hadn't shaken her unease.
``You're late.``
``I'm sorry... I got a strange phone call just as I was about to leave.``
``Phone call?``
``Yes... a silent call.@k@vS18/06/160600185. ...I could faintly hear what sounded like someone breathing, and it gave me the creeps.``
``A prank call?``
``Maybe.@k@vS18/06/160600187. But it rattled me enough to double-check all the locks before leaving, and that made me late.@k@vS18/06/160600189. I'm sorry.``
Shion dipped her head apologetically.
``But seeing your face put me right at ease, Kei-chan.@k@vS18/06/160600190. You look so utterly relaxed... heh heh heh!``
Shion laughed without a hint of guilt.
``Yeah, yeah, I know -- I look like I've got zero tension in my face.``
``Ahahaha, don't sulk~``
Thanks to Shion, the taut tension finally eased a little.
But as we climbed the stairs one by one, both Shion and I fell gradually silent, and an edge of razor-sharp intensity cut through the air.
All the actors were assembled.
Keiichi Maebara. Rena Ryuugu. Shion Sonozaki.
The three of us faced each other from a measured distance, and before us lay two cards.
The first card was Teppei Houjou.
The star of tonight's show. The lead actor, absent from the stage.
Right about now, he was probably lounging around without a care in the world, oblivious to his cue.
changeReason: Old says 'his time was quickly approaching' (vague, ominous); new says 'lounging around without a care, oblivious to his cue' matching JPN のうのうと過ごしてる (carefree/nonchalant). Different characterization of the target.

TextENGNew: The image of Teppei idling about while tormenting Satoko flashed through my mind, stoking the fire inside me.
Forcing down the roiling emotions churning within, I turned over the second card.
The second card... the perfect crime.
Those words rang with an almost magical allure.
For the sake of our 'rightful future,' we had to make the crime we were about to commit absolutely airtight.
changeReason: Old says 'this crime is necessary for our ideal future'; new adds 'make the crime absolutely airtight' matching JPN 完全なるもの. New captures the critical detail about making the crime undetectable.

TextENGNew: That was why we had gathered here -- to engineer a flawless, perfect crime, with not a single gap or weakness.
``Rena has...@k@vS18/02/160200181. done some research into planning the perfect crime.``
With that, Rena produced a college notebook crammed cover to cover with writing.
She must have poured her soul into every word -- the paper was warped and the ink smeared in places from the sheer pressure of her pen.
It made clear just how deadly serious Rena was.
``The actual method of killing doesn't really matter.@k@vS18/02/160200183. What matters is making sure no one discovers that we're the ones who did it.@k@vS18/02/160200184. That's the key.``
Indeed, the world was full of criminals who went to painstaking lengths to cover their tracks.
But most of them ended up destroying themselves in the process.
Human beings are imperfect. Small mistakes compound, dots connect into lines, and before you know it, those lines draw a path of suspicion leading straight back to you.
``In other words, we need an alibi?``
``Exactly.@k@vS18/02/160200186. That's why all of us are going to attend the Watanagashi festival tomorrow night.``
``But in reality, instead of being at the festival, we'll be killing Teppei...``
Rena responded to Shion's words with a deep nod.
``So we'll each vouch for the others' alibi?``
``Yeah.``
``Tomorrow, we'll be innocently enjoying the Watanagashi festival...@k@vS18/06/160600195. As if nothing happened at all...``
But in truth, we would be extinguishing Teppei Houjou's life with our own hands.
Just as Rena had torn the head off that stuffed animal in the wagon...
Without batting an eye... effortlessly...
``Another thing -- we need to think about eyewitnesses.``
``Most of the villagers will be at the festival.@k@vS18/02/160200189. Striking during that window should be our best bet.``
``We kill Teppei in a place where no one goes, where no one will see...``
``Anything can happen, so we need to finish it as quickly as possible.``
The plan took shape methodically, dispassionately.
This was neither a game nor a fantasy. This was real.
I needed to feel something tangible, so I quietly dug my nails into my kneecap.
A dull ache answered, grounding me.
``That takes care of the perpetrators and the witnesses...@k@vS18/02/160200192. Next, we need to erase the victim's existence...``
``Erase the victim's existence...?``
``If the victim doesn't exist, then the crime itself doesn't exist.@k@vS18/02/160200194. In other words, there's nothing for anyone to investigate.``
``You mean body disposal?``
``Yeah.@k@vS18/02/160200196. We have to hide it somewhere it will absolutely never be found.``
A place where it would never be found, for all eternity...
The gloomy vision of Onigafuchi swamp suddenly surfaced in my mind.
A bottomless swamp... It seemed like the perfect stage for Teppei to meet his end.
``What about the Onigafuchi swamp?@k@vS18/01/160100331. If we use that, no one would ever find out, would they?``
Rena grimaced at my suggestion.
``Rena thought so too at first.@k@vS18/02/160200198. But if you thought of it, then everyone else would think of it too, wouldn't they?``
``The police would suspect Onigafuchi right away.@k@vS18/02/160200200. If they ever decided to organize a full search... the body would be found immediately.``
``Yeah, you're right, Rena...``
``We also need to think about how to transport the body.``
We threw our heart and soul into refining the plan.
We'd tear apart a plan we'd just assembled and rebuild it from scratch.
After tearing down and reconstructing again and again, we settled on the simplest approach.
First, we would lure Satoko out by inviting her to the festival, leaving Teppei alone.
Then, posing as the police, we would lure Teppei outside.
He would most likely head out on his motorcycle.
There was only one road from Satoko's house to town, and it had no streetlights.
We would set an ambush in the darkness, stretching a black-painted rope across the road.
When Teppei appeared on his bike, Rena and Shion would pull both ends of the rope taut simultaneously.
Teppei would crash, bike and all.
Then I would move in instantly and deliver the killing blow.
Having asked Rena and Shion for their 'cooperation,' I wanted to carry out the act of killing with my own hands alone.
Beyond that, a sense of duty -- like a protective talisman -- compelled me not to let those two become murderers.
Both of them frowned at my proposal at first. But eventually, as if my resolve had reached them, they promised to entrust everything to me.
After the killing, the three of us would carry the body to a nearby thicket and hide it in a hole we'd dug in advance.
Shion would sink the motorcycle in Onigafuchi.
And Rena would dispose of the murder weapon.
``All that's left is the weapon...``
Confronted with the overwhelming reality that we were about to take a human life, we fell silent once more.
We stared at our own hands -- the hands that would grip the weapon.
It didn't feel real.
But oddly, my heart was calm.
The reassurance that came from not being alone.
And the diluted guilt that came from not bearing this alone.
We gazed into each other's eyes, as if confirming each other's hearts.
``A knife or a kitchen blade would be easy to get, but you'd probably get splashed with blood. I think we should avoid that.``
``Then what about strangling him with a rope?``
``If he fights back or tries to run... the risk is too high.``
``Then... what other options are there?``
I pressed Rena for an answer.
Rena looked up at the ceiling, searching for one.
``Ehehehehehe...``
...Just then, Shion began trembling, her head still bowed.
Was she crying?
``Ehehehehehe... Ahahaha, hahahahahahaha, hahahaha!!!``
Without warning, Shion burst into laughter as though a dam had broken.
changeReason: Old says 'suddenly broke into a laugh'; new adds the JPN simile 堰を切ったように 'as though a dam had broken', conveying a more intense emotional release.

TextENGNew: ``Shii-chan...?``
``What's wrong, Shion?``
``I just had... the most brilliant idea... ehehehe.``
``A brilliant idea...?``
``The perfect weapon for snuffing out that man's life.@k@vS18/06/160600203. Ehehehehehe!!@k@vS18/06/160600204. It's just too poetic!``
changeReason: Old names 'Satoshi-kun's bat' and says 'Satoshi-kun can finally be at ease'; new replaces with 'It's just too poetic!', dropping the bat identification and Satoshi's peace that are explicitly in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``It's so perfect it's hilarious!@k@vS18/06/160600208. Aaahahahahahahaha!!@k@vS18/06/160600209. Serves him right!!!!``
Shion kept laughing even as tears streamed down her face.
A shiver ran through me.
``Heh... hehehehe... heh...@k@vS18/02/160200205. Hehehehehe... ahahahahahaha!!``
Even Rena...?
``You're right!@k@vS18/02/160200207. It's sooo perfect~!@k@vS18/02/160200208. That man writhing in agony... what a fitting end!``
changeReason: Old includes detailed suffering ('struggling on the ground, pleading for help, looking down on him from above'); new condenses to 'writhing in agony... what a fitting end!' omitting significant detail present in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``Kei-chan, you should be laughing with us.``
``That's right.@k@vS18/02/160200213. That man's death is something to celebrate.``
``Come on, laugh with us, Kei-chan.``
``Laugh, Keiichi-kun.``
Bwaa-ha-hahahahahahahahaha hahahahaha hahahahahahahaaaaaaa!!!!!
I laughed from the very pit of my stomach.
I laughed until I couldn't laugh any more.
changeReason: Old says 'despite our current situation, I roared with laughter'; new says 'I laughed until I couldn't laugh anymore'. Different framing: old emphasizes incongruity, new emphasizes exhaustion.

TextENGNew: Once Teppei was dead, Satoko would be happy.
Once that thought took hold, I couldn't help finding everything hilariously funny.
And so, after laughing ourselves out,
a single tear spilled from my eye, for some reason...
Suddenly, a flat, mechanical sound rang out, crashing me back into reality.
I discreetly wiped my tears before the others could notice, then stood and left the room.
As I closed the door behind me, the lukewarm air and the familiar smell of home enveloped me.
I nearly gave in to the nostalgia and comfort of it all.
The version of me who had lived unremarkably in this house, never questioning a thing... would vanish from this world tomorrow, along with a single corpse...
``Hello? Who is this?``
On the other end, someone was holding their breath.
``Hello?@k@vS18/01/160100341. If this is a prank call, I'm hanging up.``
Without a word, the caller hung up. Feeling an unresolved discomfort, I set the receiver back down.
I turned on my heel to head back to my room--
The phone rang again. I hesitated a moment before reaching for the receiver.
I said nothing, holding my breath, trying to unmask whoever was on the line.
A voice on the verge of fading away... It was Mion.
``Mion?``
``Kei-chan?``
``Did you call just now?``
``No, I didn't...@k@vS18/03/160300103. Is something wrong?``
``No, it's nothing.@k@vS18/01/160100348. What about you?@k@vS18/01/160100349. Did something happen?``
``...Kei-chan, you're acting strange.@k@vS18/03/160300105. Isn't something going on with you?``
``...Nothing, really.``
``...If you say so...@k@vS18/03/160300107. It's about tomorrow, actually.``
``Tomorrow?``
``Yeah, the festival.``
``...The festival, huh...``
``You're being really strange... Are you sure you're okay?``
``Yeah... I just woke up, so my brain isn't working yet.``
``...Oh, you were sleeping?``
``I was out cold.@k@vS18/01/160100355. At least until you woke me up.``
``Ahahaha, sorry about that.``
Terrified of giving myself away, I concentrated intensely on every word Mion said.
changeReason: Old says 'carefully choose my responses' (active strategy) but Japanese means concentrating on Mion's words (passive listening).

TextENGNew: ``So, you're coming to the festival tomorrow, right?``
``I'm planning to, but...``
``But... what?``
``No, no, I'm definitely going!@k@vS18/01/160100358. I already promised Rena I'd go with her.``
``...Oh, you're going with Rena...``
I could picture the sadness on Mion's face on the other end of the line.
The loneliness of being left out... I understood that feeling all too well. So I hastily tried to smooth things over.
``You're going too, of course, right, Mion?@k@vS18/01/160100360. Let's all meet up there!``
``...Right. Yeah.@k@vS18/03/160300116. I was worried you might be going alone, Kei-chan...@k@vS18/03/160300117. But if Rena is with you, I'm relieved.``
``...What do you mean by that?``
``Ah, ahahaha... sorry, it's nothing.@k@vS18/03/160300119. Oh, I have to hang up now.@k@vS18/03/160300120. See you tomorrow, Kei-chan.``
Without waiting for a reply, Mion hung up. ...An unsettled discomfort lingered as I reluctantly set the receiver down.
If I'm with Rena, she's relieved...?
An odd anxiety welled up at those loaded words.
Still churning with unresolved feelings, I started back up the stairs toward my room... but my feet stopped just short of the second floor.
The door, which I was sure I'd closed... was open.
The light from my room spilled into the dim hallway, and two shadows stood silhouetted against the wall.
But the moment they sensed me, the two shadows quickly retreated back into the room.
Had they been listening to my phone conversation?
Leaning out, holding their breath... they had been watching me!
Why?! ...The answer was obvious. To 'protect our shared secret.'
In other words, Rena and Shion had been hiding in the shadows... monitoring me to make sure I didn't leak anything.
If I had slipped up... what would the two of them have...?
Rena, Shion, and me.
The bond between the three of us had become as strong as an intricately tangled chain.
It would never be undone. Our relationship would continue until the chain rusted, faded, and crumbled to dust.
But... for some reason, I couldn't shake the feeling that the two of them were drifting further and further away.
When I returned to the room, Rena and Shion greeted me as if nothing had happened.
Seeing the two of them wearing coordinated smiles only stoked my irritation further.
changeReason: Old says 'feel more anxious' but Japanese 苛立ち means 'irritation/frustration' -- different emotion.

TextENGNew: ``That phone call... who was it from? Who?``
``...Why would you ask that?``
``I was just curious.@k@vS18/02/160200221. Keiichi-kun, you look kind of scary right now...``
That's because you two were testing me! You were eavesdropping, and now you have the nerve to act innocent!
changeReason: Old says 'spying on me! Hypocrite!'; new says 'testing me! eavesdropping, act innocent!'. JPN distinguishes 試す (testing), 盗み聞き (eavesdropping), and 空々しい (feigning innocence). New captures each nuance.

TextENGNew: ``Keiichi-kun... you're not hiding anything from us, are you? Are you?``
``I'm not hiding anything.@k@vS18/01/160100364. You're the ones who were sneaking around behind my back -- what were you doing?!``
My question came out nearly as a shout. But Rena said nothing, only looking down and biting her lip.
changeReason: Old says 'asked in exasperation'; Japanese 激昂にも似た means 'resembling rage/fury' -- stronger emotion.

TextENGNew: In her place, Shion leaned forward and fixed me with a challenging stare.
``...Just what exactly are you accusing us of?``
``Don't play dumb.@k@vS18/01/160100366. You were both eavesdropping on my phone call, weren't you?!``
``Eavesdropping...?@k@vS18/06/160600218. What a terrible thing to say.@k@vS18/06/160600219. You can't seriously think that.``
``Then what was that?@k@vS18/01/160100368. You're denying it?``
``Keiichi-kun... you are getting cold feet after all...``
Rena murmured those words, then lifted her gaze to me, suspicion pooling in her eyes.
``...You were going to betray us...@k@vS18/02/160200225. You were telling someone about our plan, Keiichi-kun...``
``What?!@k@vS18/01/160100370. How did you even arrive at that conclusion?!``
``You were setting us up...@k@vS18/02/160200227. You deceived us all along...@k@vS18/02/160200228. Pretending to be on our side, just to trap us...``
Rena's delusions had spiraled completely out of control. I was too stunned for anger.
changeReason: Different framing of emotional direction: old attributes rage to speaker, new says speaker is beyond anger.

TextENGNew: ``Did you think you could outsmart me?@k@vS18/02/160200231. ...Hehehe... ahahahahahaha!@k@vS18/02/160200232. Rena was fooled! Rena was tricked! Is that it?``
changeReason: Old translates 可哀相 as 'pathetic' directed at Keiichi; new says 'Rena was fooled! Rena was tricked!' which inverts who the mockery targets.

TextENGNew: ``You're a bad boy, Keiichi-kun -- vile and filthy!@k@vS18/02/160200235. You'll be punished for this.@k@vS18/02/160200236. Oyashiro-sama will never forgive you.``
A low, rumbling laugh that seemed to shake the ground beneath my feet. Rena shot me a cutting sidelong glance, then rose to her feet.
She closed in on me like a predator cornering its prey.
``Whoooo?@k@vS18/02/160200239. Who were you talking to just now?``
Rena's wide-open eyes simmered with killing intent.
I instinctively backed away, only to find Shion standing right behind me, cutting off my retreat.
``W-what's wrong with you two...?``
``Don't tell me that phone call just now...@k@vS18/06/160600221. Was it from Sis, by any chance?``
``Mii-chan...? Why?@k@vS18/02/160200241. Why would she call?!``
``Please be quiet, Rena!``
Shion silenced Rena with a single piercing glare, then turned on me.
``What did you tell my sister?``
``I-I didn't tell her anything...@k@vS18/01/160100373. She just asked if I was going to the festival, that's all.``
``That was truly the only thing?``
I nodded frantically.
``I can trust you?``
``I haven't done a single thing to be ashamed of.@k@vS18/01/160100375. The real question is -- can I trust you two?``
Our gazes collided as each tried to read the other's true intentions.
A slight dip of the head from Rena and Shion -- was it a nod of acceptance, or a bow of resignation...?
This was no time for infighting... We had to unite and save Satoko.
We were on the verge of committing the ultimate taboo -- murder. The extreme excitement, tension, and fear had us all trembling.
Our frayed nerves had made us far more suspicious than warranted, and once we all recognized that... we agreed to take a breath and calm ourselves down.
``Still, why would she call right now...?@k@vS18/06/160600227. It's almost like she knew we were gathered here...@k@vS18/06/160600228. Suspicious, wouldn't you say?``
``Aren't you overthinking this?@k@vS18/01/160100377. Mion doesn't have clairvoyance.``
``But Sis has far more than just her own two eyes.``
``What, she's got a third eye hidden under her bangs?@k@vS18/01/160100379. You've been reading too many manga.``
``Far more than three... she has at least a hundred eyes.``
``What does that mean?@k@vS18/01/160100381. Is Mion some kind of monster...?``
``I told you before.@k@vS18/06/160600232. This is Hinamizawa.``
``So you're saying it's not just Mion herself.@k@vS18/01/160100383. ...We need to be on guard against every villager?``
``Even after everything is over tomorrow night, I think we should stay cautious for a while.``
``R-right...``
``Rena's been thinking about something...``
Having finally regained some composure, Rena cautiously spoke up, reading Shion's expression.
``Do you think it would be okay to tell Mii-chan about the plan? ...Would it?``
``Absolutely not!``
``B-but... Mii-chan is really worried about Satoko-chan too...@k@vS18/02/160200245. I think she'd understand.``

``No matter how close we are, Sis won't let personal feelings sway her.@k@vS18/06/160600236. She may not look it, but she is the head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS18/06/160600237. You'd better think of her as no different from the villagers who ostracize Satoko.``
``No, maybe even worse...@k@vS18/06/160600239. Consider her the single most dangerous person to let in on our plan.@k@vS18/06/160600240. Under no circumstances can she find out.``
Rena drew a sharp breath at those words, and the tension flooding back into her face took on a dangerous edge once more.
changeReason: Old misses 剣呑 (dangerous/menacing); new captures the threatening quality.

TextENGNew: Trembling from head to toe... she ripped a handful of tatami fibers from the mat.
changeReason: Old says trembling tore into tatami (involuntary); new says she deliberately ripped tatami fibers.

TextENGNew: ``Anyone who gets in the way... Rena will never forgive...@k@vS18/02/160200247. Never forgive...@k@vS18/02/160200248. Absolutely never...``
No matter what happened, we could not let the plan fail. Even if it meant betraying the friends we'd had until now.
We stared into each other's eyes, as if branding the resolve into one another's hearts.
After a time, Shion picked up the college notebook that had been set aside. And then--
``We need to dispose of this...``
We nodded in agreement and headed to the parlor.
On the table sat a large, dust-covered ashtray.
*Riiip*... *Rip rip*...
Rena, her face blank, tore the notebook apart page by page.
*Riiip*... *Rip rip*...
Shion then took the scattered fragments and shredded them even smaller.
I gathered the scraps and piled them in the ashtray, then struck a match.
A single thread of smoke curled upward as a small, pale flame began to burn.
The flame crept outward, slowly swallowing the white paper beneath a spreading tide of black.
With murderous intent blazing in our hearts, we sat in silence,
holding our breath as we watched it slowly transform to ash.
All for the sake of our 'rightful future.' ...That was the only thought any of us held.
When everything had burned away, only dark, blackened ash remained.
It was like the embodiment of the mutual suspicion none of us had been able to shake.
As if to smother the last ember, Shion slowly filled the ashtray with water.
The die was cast.
We had no choice but to kill...
Alone in my room after Rena and Shion had left, I tried to fall asleep.
The sleepless nights had continued for days now. But after tomorrow, these bleak, restless evenings would be over.
I clenched my fist.
The sensation of gripping a bat spread through my entire body.
With these hands... I will kill him!
When I held my palm up toward the darkness, I thought I could glimpse, through the gaps between my fingers, a future radiant with blinding light.
And so, my entire being savored a sense of fulfillment unlike anything I had ever known...
